sanskrit:
तस्मादज्ञानसम्भूतं हृत्स्थं ज्ञानासिनात्मनः।
छित्त्वैनं संशयं योगमातिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ भारत।।
transliteration:
tasmād ajñāna-sambhūtaṃ hṛt-sthaṃ jñānāsinātmanaḥ |
chittvainaṃ saṃśayaṃ yogam ātiṣṭhottiṣṭha bhārata ||
literal_meaning: Therefore, the doubt born of ignorance, seated in the heart, sever it with the sword of self-knowledge; established in yoga, arise, O Bhārata.
contextual_meaning: Krishna ends the chapter by prescribing a direct cognitive-behavioral intervention: use discriminating knowledge as a scalpel to excise the irrational doubt that paralyzes action.
psychological_state: Decisional paralysis rooted in catastrophizing and moral fusion; needs cognitive disputation and values-based exposure.
trigger_scenario: A junior surgeon freezes before a critical operation, overwhelmed by ‘what-ifs’; the senior commands: ‘Trust your training, pick up the scalpel, and cut—starting with your own fear.’
anchor_text: Cut through self-doubt with clear knowledge and act.
